note: Rome I Regulation (EC) No 593/2008, Article 6, prevents a choice-of-law clause from depriving a consumer of the protections of the law of their habitual residence, regardless of the law chosen.

California Labor Code § 925 voids clauses requiring a California employee to litigate or arbitrate employment disputes outside California, or under another state's law, as a condition of employment (with a narrow carve-out for individually-negotiated agreements with counsel).
The same California Labor Code § 925 restriction applies to exclusive-venue clauses in employment agreements, not just choice-of-law clauses.
